identificationModule.briefTitle: Exenatide Versus Glimepiride in Patients With Type 2 Diabetes
identificationModule.officialTitle: Long Term Treatment With Exenatide Versus Glimepiride in Patients With Type 2 Diabetes Pretreated With Metformin (EUREXA: European Exenatide Study)

descriptionModule.briefSummary: This study assesses the effects of twice-daily subcutaneous injection exenatide versus treatment with sulfonylurea (glimepiride) on long-term glycemic control and beta-cell function.

interventions.name: exenatide
interventions.description: subcutaneous injection (5mcg or 10mcg), twice a day

interventions.name: glimepiride
interventions.description: oral tablet (titrated to maximally tolerated dose), once daily

primaryOutcomes.measure: Number of Patients With Treatment Failure
primaryOutcomes.description: Treatment failure is defined as one of the following:1. HbA1c exceeding 9% at any visit after the initial 3 months of treatment (i.e., earliest at Month 6), on the maximally tolerated dose of antidiabetic agents. 2. HbA1c exceeding 7% at 2 consecutive visits 3 months apart, after the initial 6 months of treatment (i.e., earliest at Month 9), on the maximally tolerated dose of antidiabetic agents.
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Baseline to end of Period II (up to 4.5 years)

primaryOutcomes.description: Treatment failure is defined as one of the following:1. HbA1c exceeding 9% at any visit after the initial 3 months of treatment (i.e., earliest at Month 6), on the maximally tolerated dose of antidiabetic agents. 2. HbA1c exceeding 7% at 2 consecutive visits 3 months apart, after the initial 6 months of treatment (i.e., earliest at Month 9), on the maximally tolerated dose of antidiabetic agents.
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Baseline to end of Period II (up to 4.5 years)

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Homeostasis Model Assessment of Beta-cell Function (HOMA-B) at Year 3
secondaryOutcomes.description: HOMA-B at Year 3. HOMA-B is an index of beta-cell function and was calculated as: HOMA-B = (20 x fasting insulin (measured in pmol/L))/((fasting glucose (measured in mmol/L) - 3.5) x 7.175).

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in HOMA-B From Baseline to Endpoint
secondaryOutcomes.description: Change in HOMA-B from baseline to endpoint.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Baseline, end of Period II (up to 4.5 years)

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Fasting Proinsulin/Insulin Ratio at Year 3
secondaryOutcomes.description: Fasting proinsulin (measured in pmol/L)/insulin (measured in pmol/L) ratio at Year 3.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in Fasting Proinsulin/Insulin Ratio From Baseline to Endpoint.
secondaryOutcomes.description: Change in fasting proinsulin (measured in pmol/L)/insulin (measured in pmol/L) ratio from baseline to endpoint.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Baseline, end of Period II (up to 4.5 years)

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Ratio of the 30 Minute Increment in Plasma Insulin Concentration and the 30 Minute Increment in Plasma Glucose During the Oral Glucose Tolerance Test (DI30/DG30 Ratio) at Year 3
secondaryOutcomes.description: DI30/DG30 at Year 3. DI30/DG30 ratio was calculated as (30 minute post prandial insulin - fasting insulin) (measured in pmol/L)/(30 minute post prandial glucose - fasting glucose) (measured in mmol/L).

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in DI30/DG30 Ratio From Baseline to Endpoint
secondaryOutcomes.description: Change in DI30/DG30 ratio from baseline to endpoint.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Baseline, end of Period II (up to 4.5 years)

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Disposition Index at Year 3
secondaryOutcomes.description: Disposition Index at Year 3. Disposition index was calculated as (DI30/DG30 ratio)/(HOMA index for insulin resistance (HOMA-IR)); where HOMA-IR=(fasting insulin (measured in pmol/L) x fasting glucose (measured in mmol/L))/(22.5 x 7.175).

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in Disposition Index From Baseline to Endpoint
secondaryOutcomes.description: Change in disposition index from baseline to endpoint.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Baseline, end of Period II (up to 4.5 years)

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in HbA1c From Baseline to Year 3
secondaryOutcomes.description: Change in HbA1c from baseline to Year 3.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in HbA1c From Baseline to Endpoint
secondaryOutcomes.description: Change in HbA1c from baseline to endpoint. Endpoint for HbA1c was defined as the HbA1c measured at the treatment failure for patients reaching primary endpoint and was the last observation in study period II for other patients (either followed until the end of the study period II or discontinuing the study).
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Baseline, end of Period II (up to 4.5 years)

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Fasting Plasma Glucose at Year 3
secondaryOutcomes.description: Fasting plasma glucose at Year 3.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in Fasting Plasma Glucose From Baseline to Endpoint
secondaryOutcomes.description: Change in fasting plasma glucose from baseline to endpoint.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Baseline, end of Period II (up to 4.5 years)

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Postprandial (2 Hours) Plasma Glucose at Year 3
secondaryOutcomes.description: Postprandial (2 hours) plasma glucose at Year 3.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in Postprandial (2 Hours) Plasma Glucose From Baseline to Endpoint
secondaryOutcomes.description: Change from baseline in postprandial (2 hours) plasma glucose to endpoint.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Baseline, end of Period II (up to 4.5 years)

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in Body Weight From Baseline to Year 3
secondaryOutcomes.description: Change in Body weight from baseline to Year 3.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Systolic Blood Pressure at Year 3
secondaryOutcomes.description: Systolic Blood pressure at Year 3.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Diastolic Blood Pressure at Year 3
secondaryOutcomes.description: Diastolic Blood pressure at Year 3.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Heart Rate at Year 3
secondaryOutcomes.description: Heart rate at Year 3.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Triglycerides at Year 3
secondaryOutcomes.description: Triglycerides at Year 3.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Total Cholesterol at Year 3
secondaryOutcomes.description: Total Cholesterol at Year 3.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: High-density Lipoprotein (HDL) Cholesterol at Year 3
secondaryOutcomes.description: HDL Cholesterol at Year 3.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Hypoglycemia Rate Per Year
secondaryOutcomes.description: All hypoglycemia episodes were taken into account. Severe hypoglycemia: event requiring assistance of another person to administer carbohydrate, glucagons, or other resuscitative actions; Documented symptomatic hypoglycemia: event with typical symptoms accompanied by a measured plasma glucose concentration \<=70 mg/dL; Asymptomatic hypoglycemia: event not accompanied by typical symptoms but with a measured plasma glucose concentration \<=70 mg/dL; Probable symptomatic hypoglycemia: event with symptoms not accompanied by a plasma glucose determination.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Baseline to end of Period II (up to 4.5 years)

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in HbA1c From Baseline to Year 2 for Patients Randomized at Entry in Period III
secondaryOutcomes.description: Change in HbA1c from baseline to Year 2.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Baseline in Period III, Year 2 in Period III

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in HbA1c From Baseline to Year 2 for Patients Not Randomized at Entry in Period III
secondaryOutcomes.description: Change in HbA1c from baseline to Year 2.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Baseline in Period III, Year 2 in Period III

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Hypoglycemia Rate Per Year in Period III
secondaryOutcomes.description: All hypoglycemia episodes were taken into account. Severe hypoglycemia: event requiring assistance of another person to administer carbohydrate, glucagons, or other resuscitative actions; Documented symptomatic hypoglycemia: event with typical symptoms accompanied by a measured plasma glucose concentration \<=70 mg/dL; Asymptomatic hypoglycemia: event not accompanied by typical symptoms but with a measured plasma glucose concentration \<=70 mg/dL; Probable symptomatic hypoglycemia: event with symptoms not accompanied by a plasma glucose determination.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Start of Period III to end of study

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Diagnosed with type 2 diabetes mellitus.
* Treated with diet and exercise and a stable, maximally tolerated dose of metformin for at least 3 months prior to screening.
* HbA1c \>=6.5% and \<=9.0%.
* Body Mass Index (BMI) \>=25 kg/m\^2 and \<40 kg/m\^2.

Exclusion Criteria:

* Participated in an interventional medical, surgical, or pharmaceutical study within 30 days prior to screening.
* Characteristics contraindicating metformin or glimepiride use.
* Receiving drugs that directly affect gastrointestinal motility.
* Receiving chronic (lasting longer than 2 weeks) systemic glucocorticoid therapy.
* Have used any prescription drug to promote weight loss within 3 months prior to screening.
* Treated for longer than 2 weeks with any of the following medications within 3 months prior to screening: \*insulin; \*thiazolidinediones; \*alpha-glucosidase inhibitors; \*sulfonylurea; \*meglitinides

references.citation: Simo R, Guerci B, Schernthaner G, Gallwitz B, Rosas-Guzman J, Dotta F, Festa A, Zhou M, Kiljanski J. Long-term changes in cardiovascular risk markers during administration of exenatide twice daily or glimepiride: results from the European exenatide study. Cardiovasc Diabetol. 2015 Sep 4;14:116. doi: 10.1186/s12933-015-0279-z.

Schernthaner G, Rosas-Guzman J, Dotta F, Guerci B, Simo R, Festa A, Kiljanski J, Zhou M, Gallwitz B. Treatment escalation options for patients with type 2 diabetes after failure of exenatide twice daily or glimepiride added to metformin: results from the prospective European Exenatide (EUREXA) study. Diabetes Obes Metab. 2015 Jul;17(7):689-98. doi: 10.1111/dom.12471. Epub 2015 May 8.

Gallwitz B, Guzman J, Dotta F, Guerci B, Simo R, Basson BR, Festa A, Kiljanski J, Sapin H, Trautmann M, Schernthaner G. Exenatide twice daily versus glimepiride for prevention of glycaemic deterioration in patients with type 2 diabetes with metformin failure (EUREXA): an open-label, randomised controlled trial. Lancet. 2012 Jun 16;379(9833):2270-8. doi: 10.1016/S0140-6736(12)60479-6. Epub 2012 Jun 9.

participantFlowModule.preAssignmentDetails: Patients meeting defined failure of HbA1c control (primary endpoint) in Study Period II were eligible for entry to Study Period III

groups.title: Period II, Glim + Met
groups.description: Glimepiride one 1 mg tablet daily with subsequent titration every 4 weeks to maximally-tolerated dose for the remainder of Period II and daily oral Metformin

groups.title: Period III, Exen + Met + Glim - Randomized
groups.description: Glimepiride once daily, started at 1 mg dose and titrated up to maintenance doses, was added to Exenatide 10 mcg twice daily subcutaneously injected and daily oral Metformin in Period III

groups.title: Period III, Exen + Met + Pio or Rosi - Randomized
groups.description: Oral Rosiglitazone or Pioglitazone once or twice daily, started 15 mg per day, was added to Exenatide 10 mcg twice daily subcutaneously injected and daily oral Metformin in Period III

groups.title: Period III, Glim + Met + Exen - Not Randomized
groups.description: Exenatide 10 mcg twice daily subcutaneously injected (5 mcg exenatide per dose for the first 4 weeks followed by 10 mcg exenatide per dose for the remainder of period III) was added to oral Glimepiride once daily and daily oral Metformin in Period III

groups.title: Period II, Exen + Met
groups.description: Exenatide 10 mcg twice daily subcutaneously injected (5 mcg exenatide per dose for first 4 weeks followed by 10 mcg exenatide per dose for the remainder of period II) and daily oral Metformin

groups.title: Exen + Met
groups.description: Exenatide 10 mcg twice daily subcutaneously injected (5 mcg exenatide per dose for first 4 weeks followed by 10 mcg exenatide per dose for the remainder of period II) and daily oral Metformin

groups.title: Glim + Met
groups.description: Glimepiride one 1 mg tablet daily with subsequent titration every 4 weeks to maximally-tolerated dose for the remainder of Period II and daily oral Metformin

groups.title: Total
groups.description: Total of all reporting groups

outcomeMeasures.title: Number of Patients With Treatment Failure
outcomeMeasures.description: Treatment failure is defined as one of the following:1. HbA1c exceeding 9% at any visit after the initial 3 months of treatment (i.e., earliest at Month 6), on the maximally tolerated dose of antidiabetic agents. 2. HbA1c exceeding 7% at 2 consecutive visits 3 months apart, after the initial 6 months of treatment (i.e., earliest at Month 9), on the maximally tolerated dose of antidiabetic agents.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: ITT Efficacy Population: Enrolled patients with a baseline and at least one post-baseline measurement of HbA1c in Study Period II (including only Study Period II); patients analyzed according to treatment as randomized.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: Baseline to end of Period II (up to 4.5 years)

groups.title: Exen + Met
groups.description: Exenatide 10 mcg twice daily subcutaneously injected (5 mcg exenatide per dose for first 4 weeks followed by 10 mcg exenatide per dose for the remainder of period II) and daily oral Metformin

groups.title: Glim + Met
groups.description: Glimepiride one 1 mg tablet daily with subsequent titration every 4 weeks to maximally-tolerated dose for the remainder of Period II and daily oral Metformin

classes.title: Number of patients with treatment failure

analyses.groupDescription: The null hypothesis (H0) and alternative hypothesis (H1) for the primary analysis (i.e., non-inferiority) are:H0: hazards of treatment for Exenatide/hazards of treatment for Glimepiride \>=1.25.H1: hazards of treatment for Exenatide/hazards of treatment for Glimepiride \< 1.25. With 527 patients in each arm, the study would have approximately 90% power to conclude non-inferiority of Exenatide to Glimepiride.
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non inferiority test was based on the upper 1-sided 97.5% CI for the hazard ratio of Exenatide/Glimepiride;the upper bound was compared to 1.25: if \<1.25, the hypothesis that the risk of treatment failure with Exenatide is more than 1.25 times greater than the risk with Glimepiride is rejected.Superiority test was based on the 2-sided 95% CI for the hazard ratio. If CI excludes 1, the hypothesis that the risk of treatment failure with Exenatide is equal to that with Glimepiride is rejected.
analyses.statisticalComment: Time to treatment failure was modeled using Cox regression with treatment and baseline HbA1c as predictive terms.

outcomeMeasures.title: Time to Treatment Failure
outcomeMeasures.description: Treatment failure is defined as one of the following:1. HbA1c exceeding 9% at any visit after the initial 3 months of treatment (i.e., earliest at Month 6), on the maximally tolerated dose of antidiabetic agents. 2. HbA1c exceeding 7% at 2 consecutive visits 3 months apart, after the initial 6 months of treatment (i.e., earliest at Month 9), on the maximally tolerated dose of antidiabetic agents.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: ITT Efficacy Population.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: Baseline to end of Period II (up to 4.5 years)

groups.title: Exen + Met
groups.description: Exenatide 10 mcg twice daily subcutaneously injected (5 mcg exenatide per dose for first 4 weeks followed by 10 mcg exenatide per dose for the remainder of period II) and daily oral Metformin

groups.title: Glim + Met
groups.description: Glimepiride one 1 mg tablet daily with subsequent titration every 4 weeks to maximally-tolerated dose for the remainder of Period II and daily oral Metformin

measurements.comment: Since the last observed time point, beyond which all are missing, is still within the 95% confidence interval for the median survival time, the upper bound should be no less than the last observed time point, and is unknown due to censoring.

analyses.groupDescription: Kaplan-Meier survival curves for time to treatment failure were compared between treatment groups using log rank test.

outcomeMeasures.title: Homeostasis Model Assessment of Beta-cell Function (HOMA-B) at Year 3
outcomeMeasures.description: HOMA-B at Year 3. HOMA-B is an index of beta-cell function and was calculated as: HOMA-B = (20 x fasting insulin (measured in pmol/L))/((fasting glucose (measured in mmol/L) - 3.5) x 7.175).
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: ITT Efficacy Population. The analysis included only time points up to that week where at least 25% of the originally enrolled population was still in the study. Missing data at Year 3 was not imputed.

groups.title: Exen + Met
groups.description: Exenatide 10 mcg twice daily subcutaneously injected (5 mcg exenatide per dose for first 4 weeks followed by 10 mcg exenatide per dose for the remainder of period II) and daily oral Metformin

groups.title: Glim + Met
groups.description: Glimepiride one 1 mg tablet daily with subsequent titration every 4 weeks to maximally-tolerated dose for the remainder of Period II and daily oral Metformin

analyses.groupDescription: Mixed-model Repeated Measures (MMRM) analysis includes treatment, visit, and treatment by visit interaction, and baseline value as a covariate. The unstructured covariance matrix was used.

outcomeMeasures.title: Change in HOMA-B From Baseline to Endpoint
outcomeMeasures.description: Change in HOMA-B from baseline to endpoint.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: ITT Efficacy Population. Missing data at endpoint was imputed using last observation carried forward (LOCF) approach.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: Baseline, end of Period II (up to 4.5 years)

groups.title: Exen + Met
groups.description: Exenatide 10 mcg twice daily subcutaneously injected (5 mcg exenatide per dose for first 4 weeks followed by 10 mcg exenatide per dose for the remainder of period II) and daily oral Metformin

groups.title: Glim + Met
groups.description: Glimepiride one 1 mg tablet daily with subsequent titration every 4 weeks to maximally-tolerated dose for the remainder of Period II and daily oral Metformin

analyses.groupDescription: Change in HOMA-B from baseline to endpoint was analyzed by an analysis of covariance (ANCOVA) model that includes treatment and baseline HbA1c stratum (\<=7.3%, \>7.3% to \<=8.2%, \>8.2%) as factors and baseline value as a covariate.

outcomeMeasures.title: Fasting Proinsulin/Insulin Ratio at Year 3
outcomeMeasures.description: Fasting proinsulin (measured in pmol/L)/insulin (measured in pmol/L) ratio at Year 3.

outcomeMeasures.title: Change in Body Weight From Baseline to Year 3
outcomeMeasures.description: Change in Body weight from baseline to Year 3.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: ITT Safety Population: Enrolled patients receiving at least one dose of study medication in Study Period II with patients analyzed according to treatment actually received. The analysis included only time points up to that week where at least 25% of the originally enrolled population was still in the study. Missing data at Year 3 was not imputed.

groups.title: Exen + Met
groups.description: Exenatide 10 mcg twice daily subcutaneously injected (5 mcg exenatide per dose for first 4 weeks followed by 10 mcg exenatide per dose for the remainder of period II) and daily oral Metformin

groups.title: Glim + Met
groups.description: Glimepiride one 1 mg tablet daily with subsequent titration every 4 weeks to maximally-tolerated dose for the remainder of Period II and daily oral Metformin

analyses.groupDescription: MMRM analysis includes treatment, visit, and treatment by visit interaction and baseline value as a covariate. The unstructured covariance matrix was used.

outcomeMeasures.title: Hypoglycemia Rate Per Year
outcomeMeasures.description: All hypoglycemia episodes were taken into account. Severe hypoglycemia: event requiring assistance of another person to administer carbohydrate, glucagons, or other resuscitative actions; Documented symptomatic hypoglycemia: event with typical symptoms accompanied by a measured plasma glucose concentration \<=70 mg/dL; Asymptomatic hypoglycemia: event not accompanied by typical symptoms but with a measured plasma glucose concentration \<=70 mg/dL; Probable symptomatic hypoglycemia: event with symptoms not accompanied by a plasma glucose determination.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: ITT Safety Population.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: Baseline to end of Period II (up to 4.5 years)

groups.title: Exen + Met
groups.description: Exenatide 10 mcg twice daily subcutaneously injected (5 mcg exenatide per dose for first 4 weeks followed by 10 mcg exenatide per dose for the remainder of period II) and daily oral Metformin

groups.title: Glim + Met
groups.description: Glimepiride one 1 mg tablet daily with subsequent titration every 4 weeks to maximally-tolerated dose for the remainder of Period II and daily oral Metformin

analyses.groupDescription: The number of hypoglycemic episodes by patient were compared between treatment groups using a negative binomial model with effects for treatment and baseline HbA1c and the logarithm of the days of exposure as the offset variable.

outcomeMeasures.title: Change in HbA1c From Baseline to Year 2 for Patients Randomized at Entry in Period III
outcomeMeasures.description: Change in HbA1c from baseline to Year 2.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: Extension ITT Efficacy Population: Extension enrolled patients with at least one post-baseline measurement of HbA1c in Study Period III. The analysis included patients randomized at entry in study period III. Missing data at Year 2 was not imputed.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: Baseline in Period III, Year 2 in Period III

groups.title: Exen + Met + Glim - Randomized
groups.description: Glimepiride once daily, started at 1 mg dose and titrated up to maintenance doses, was added to Exenatide 10 mcg twice daily subcutaneously injected and daily oral Metformin in Period III

groups.title: Exen + Met + Pio or Rosi - Randomized
groups.description: Oral Rosiglitazone or Pioglitazone once or twice daily, started 15 mg per day, was added to Exenatide 10 mcg twice daily subcutaneously injected and daily oral Metformin in Period III

analyses.groupDescription: MMRM analysis includes treatment, visit, and treatment by visit interaction, and baseline value at Period III as a covariate. The compound symmetric covariance structure was assumed.

outcomeMeasures.title: Change in HbA1c From Baseline to Year 2 for Patients Not Randomized at Entry in Period III
outcomeMeasures.description: Change in HbA1c from baseline to Year 2.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: Extension ITT Efficacy population. The analysis included patients not randomized at entry in study period III. Missing data at Year 2 was not imputed.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: Baseline in Period III, Year 2 in Period III

groups.title: Glim + Met + Exen - Not Randomized
groups.description: Exenatide 10 mcg twice daily subcutaneously injected (5 mcg exenatide per dose for the first 4 weeks followed by 10 mcg exenatide per dose for the remainder of period III) was added to oral Glimepiride once daily and daily oral Metformin in Period III

outcomeMeasures.title: Hypoglycemia Rate Per Year in Period III
outcomeMeasures.description: All hypoglycemia episodes were taken into account. Severe hypoglycemia: event requiring assistance of another person to administer carbohydrate, glucagons, or other resuscitative actions; Documented symptomatic hypoglycemia: event with typical symptoms accompanied by a measured plasma glucose concentration \<=70 mg/dL; Asymptomatic hypoglycemia: event not accompanied by typical symptoms but with a measured plasma glucose concentration \<=70 mg/dL; Probable symptomatic hypoglycemia: event with symptoms not accompanied by a plasma glucose determination.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: Extension ITT Safety Population: Extension enrolled patients receiving at least one dose of study medication in Study Period III.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: Start of Period III to end of study

groups.title: Exen + Metformin + Glim - Randomized
groups.description: Glimepiride once daily, started at 1 mg dose and titrated up to maintenance doses, was added to Exenatide 10 mcg twice daily subcutaneously injected and daily oral Metformin in Period III

groups.title: Exen + Met + Pio or Rosi - Randomized
groups.description: Oral Rosiglitazone or Pioglitazone once or twice daily, started 15 mg per day, was added to Exenatide 10 mcg twice daily subcutaneously injected and daily oral Metformin in Period III

groups.title: Glim + Met + Exen - Not Randomized
groups.description: Exenatide 10 mcg twice daily subcutaneously injected (5 mcg exenatide per dose for the first 4 weeks followed by 10 mcg exenatide per dose for the remainder of period III) was added to oral Glimepiride once daily and daily oral Metformin in Period III

eventGroups.title: Period II, Glim + Met
eventGroups.description: Glimepiride one 1 mg tablet daily with subsequent titration every 4 weeks to maximally-tolerated dose for the remainder of Period II and daily oral Metformin

eventGroups.title: Period III, Exen + Met + Glim - Randomized
eventGroups.description: Glimepiride once daily, started at 1 mg dose and titrated up to maintenance doses, was added to Exenatide 10 mcg twice daily subcutaneously injected and daily oral Metformin in Period III

eventGroups.title: Period III, Exen + Met + Pio or Rosi - Randomized
eventGroups.description: Oral Rosiglitazone or Pioglitazone once or twice daily, started 15 mg per day, was added to Exenatide 10 mcg twice daily subcutaneously injected and daily oral Metformin in Period III

eventGroups.title: Period III, Glim + Met + Exen - Not Randomized
eventGroups.description: Exenatide 10 mcg twice daily subcutaneously injected (5 mcg exenatide per dose for the first 4 weeks followed by 10 mcg exenatide per dose for the remainder of period III) was added to oral Glimepiride once daily and daily oral Metformin in Period III

eventGroups.title: Period II, Exen + Met
eventGroups.description: Exenatide 10 mcg twice daily subcutaneously injected (5 mcg exenatide per dose for first 4 weeks followed by 10 mcg exenatide per dose for the remainder of period II) and daily oral Metformin
